summary.ascii_graph:
*-------------.   Visit each of the following eight Wikipedia pages and extract the two specific facts listed for each topic.  Present your findings as a structured fact matrix (table) with one row per topic and columns for each fact.  Cite every Wikipedia URL.

1. Great Wall of China: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China — Extract: (a) total length in km or miles AND (b) the dynasty that built the most famous sections
2. CRISPR gene editing: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing — Extract: (a) the protein used as molecular scissors AND (b) the year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells
3. Mariana Trench: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench — Extract: (a) maximum depth in metres or feet AND (b) name of the deepest point
4. Voyager 1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1 — Extract: (a) launch year AND (b) the boundary it crossed to enter interstellar space
5. Rosetta Stone: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone — Extract: (a) the three scripts on the stone AND (b) the year it was discovered
6. Penicillin: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin — Extract: (a) who discovered penicillin AND (b) the year it was discovered
7. Aurora (astronomy): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy) — Extract: (a) the scientific name for the northern lights AND (b) what causes auroras (solar particles interacting with …)
8. Enigma machine: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine — Extract: (a) who led the British effort to crack Enigma AND (b) the war in which Enigma was used

After gathering all facts, present a MARKDOWN TABLE with columns: Topic | Fact A | Fact B | Source URL

final_output.final_deliverable:
| Topic | Fact A | Fact B | Source URL |
|---|---|---|---|
| Great Wall of China | **Total length:** **21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)**. Evidence: the infobox lists **“Size 21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)”** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China). | **Dynasty that built the most famous sections:** **Ming dynasty**. Evidence: **“the best-known sections were built by the Ming dynasty (1368–1644).”** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China |
| CRISPR gene editing | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** The required quotes/facts (protein used as molecular scissors; year first used to edit mammalian cells) are not present in the supplied page text for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing. The MERGED RESULTS/RAW VISIT CONTENT did not include this page’s content. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** (See Fact A note.) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing |
| Mariana Trench | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** Although the MERGED RESULTS show a visit to an *oldid* version (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mariana_Trench&oldid=1334269865), its RAW VISIT CONTENT text was not included here, so I cannot quote the maximum depth or deepest point name. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench |
| Voyager 1 | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** No Voyager 1 page text was provided, so I cannot quote the launch year or the boundary crossed to enter interstellar space. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1 |
| Rosetta Stone | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** No Rosetta Stone page text was provided, so I cannot quote the three scripts or discovery year. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone |
| Penicillin | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** The Penicillin page was not visited in the execution trail (node skipped), and no RAW VISIT CONTENT is present to quote who discovered it and the discovery year. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin |
| Aurora (astronomy) | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** The Aurora (astronomy) page was not visited in the execution trail (node skipped), and no RAW VISIT CONTENT is present to quote the scientific name for northern lights. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy) |
| Enigma machine | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** The Enigma machine page was not visited in the execution trail (node skipped), and no RAW VISIT CONTENT is present to quote who led the British effort to crack Enigma and the war in which it was used. | **Missing evidence in provided RAW VISIT CONTENT.** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine |

## Notes on missing items (why they are missing)
Only one Wikipedia page’s full content was actually provided in **RAW VISIT CONTENT**: **Great Wall of China** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China). The execution trail shows multiple other visit tasks were **skipped**, and for Mariana Trench the system visited related pages/oldid but did not supply their full page text in the RAW VISIT CONTENT block; therefore I cannot provide the required verbatim quotes for those topics.

## Verbatim evidence quotes used (from provided RAW VISIT CONTENT)
From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China:
- Length (infobox): **“Size 21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)”**
- Dynasty for best-known sections: **“the best-known sections were built by the Ming dynasty (1368–1644).”**
final_output.action_summary: A fact matrix was produced, but only the Great Wall of China row could be fully completed with verbatim quoted evidence from the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT. All other requested topics are marked missing because their Wikipedia page text was not included in the supplied evidence (most visit nodes were skipped and/or RAW VISIT CONTENT for those URLs was not provided).
